title: Spec-Driven Development Methods
abstract: An overview of emerging spec-driven development (SDD) frameworks and tools — BMAD Method, OpenSpec, GitHub Spec Kit — that structure AI-assisted development around specifications rather than ad-hoc prompts.
why_we_care: As AI coding assistants become mainstream, vibe coding (unstructured prompting) produces inconsistent results and technical debt. Spec-driven development methods promise to bring rigour back to AI-assisted engineering by enforcing structured specifications, acceptance criteria, and implementation plans before code generation. Understanding the emerging frameworks in this space positions us to adopt the right methodology when the time comes.
summary: Several open-source frameworks are emerging around the concept of spec-driven development (SDD) — a structured approach where specifications define the “what” before the “how”. Notable projects include the BMAD Method (AI-driven agile development with specialised agents), Fission-AI's OpenSpec (SDD for AI coding assistants), and GitHub's Spec Kit (specification-as-code toolkit with broad agent support). This intell tracks their existence and evolution for potential future adoption.
description:
**What is Spec-Driven Development?**

Spec-driven development (SDD) flips traditional coding on its head: instead of diving straight into implementation with AI assistants, you first create structured specifications that define requirements, acceptance criteria, and architecture. The AI then generates code from these specs rather than from ad-hoc prompts. This approach reduces hallucinations, improves consistency, and creates auditable documentation.

**Key Frameworks**

**BMAD Method** (Build More Architect Dreams)
- AI-driven agile development framework with 12+ specialised agent personas (PM, Architect, Developer, UX, Scrum Master, etc.)
- Scale-adaptive intelligence that adjusts from bug fixes to enterprise systems
- Structured workflows grounded in agile best practices
- 40k+ GitHub stars, very active community
- Fully open source, no paywalls

**OpenSpec** (Fission-AI)
- SDD toolkit specifically designed for AI coding assistants
- Focus on engineering-grade spec creation with planning and SDLC integration
- Open source, TypeScript-based

**GitHub Spec Kit**
- Official GitHub project (76k+ stars)
- Provides slash commands: /speckit.constitution, /speckit.specify, /speckit.plan, /speckit.tasks, /speckit.implement
- Supports 20+ AI agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini, Kiro, Codex, Windsurf, etc.)
- Includes a CLI tool (specify) for project initialisation
- Both greenfield and brownfield development workflows

**Why This Matters for Us**

These tools and methods are still maturing but gaining rapid traction. They are particularly relevant because:
- They impact not just developers but also product analysts and testers (who define specs)
- They complement our ongoing evaluations of Claude Code and Kiro
- They could become standard practice for AI-assisted development within the next year

This intell is on hold — we are tracking these frameworks to stay informed, with the option to evaluate them more deeply if our AI coding tool evaluations point toward SDD as a preferred methodology.
